scope: Defensive audit of production deployment safety (DEPLOY) and environment/secret hygiene (SECRETS). Direct follow-up to the Phase-5 OBS-1 (Sentry) and CRYPTO-11 (per-landlord secrets) work — both ship a runtime hook that only earns its keep when the production deploy pipeline actually configures it. This phase audits the seam between repo state and production state.

context_baseline.do_not_reflag: Findings duplicating items already closed in phases 1-5 or 10A/10B. CRYPTO-12 (Reverb env placeholder) is already addressed; this phase extends the same pattern to the rest of .env.example without re-flagging the Reverb item.

qualityStandards.test: All Phase-11 fixes must pass `php artisan test --parallel` and `vendor/bin/pint --test`.
qualityStandards.ci: Each commit must land on main with green CI before the next phase starts.
qualityStandards.no_breaking_change: Adding production-config validators must NOT break local dev or CI. Validators throw only in app()->environment('production'); CI runs as 'testing'.

DEPLOY.description: Production deployment safety — workflow automation, migration rehearsal, cache warming, queue restarts, health-check coverage, runbook gaps. The repo has zero deploy automation today; every production cutover is a human shell session.

SECRETS.description: Environment and secret hygiene — production-config validators that fail closed, .env.example defaults that are unsafe to ship as-is, per-landlord secret rotation procedures, missing breach-readiness contact info.

items.title: No deploy workflow exists — production cutover is fully manual
items.evidence: ls .github/workflows -> ci.yml only. No deploy.yml, no production.yml, no Dockerfile, no deploy/* directory. Every production deploy is a manual SSH session running `git pull && composer install --no-dev && php artisan migrate && php artisan queue:restart` (best case). No audit trail of who deployed which commit when; no rollback automation; no pre-deploy gate (lint+tests already green on the deployed SHA).
items.fix: Add .github/workflows/deploy.yml triggered on push to main, gated on CI success (workflow_run dependency). Steps: SSH to server (action secrets), checkout deploy SHA, run a project deploy script that runs migrate --force + config/route/event cache + queue:restart + restart php-fpm. Annotate deploys via gh deployment API so the GitHub UI shows current production SHA.

items.title: validateProductionSecurity logs warnings instead of failing fast on critical misconfig
items.evidence: Empty APP_KEY -> Log::channel('security')->warning only; the app boots and serves traffic with encrypted columns unreadable. SESSION_ENCRYPT=false, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=false, HSTS disabled all log-only. The only THROW is reached when APP_DEBUG=true AND environment is production (line 506). Critical-but-not-debug misconfigs ship silently.
items.fix: Split the validator into 'critical' (empty APP_KEY, missing session.encrypt) and 'warning' (HSTS off, etc.) tiers. Critical tier throws RuntimeException at boot; warning tier logs. Add a SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN empty check at the warning tier so OBS-1 silent no-op is visible.

items.title: Sentry DSN not validated at boot — OBS-1 silently no-ops in production
items.evidence: Phase-5 OBS-1 installed sentry-laravel and wired DomainException::report. SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN is intentionally blank in .env.example. Nothing checks that production has a real DSN — a deploy that forgets to set it ships error monitoring as a silent no-op. ops cannot answer 'why didn't we get a Sentry alert for the last incident'.
items.fix: In validateProductionSecurity, warn (and ideally throw via the critical tier) when SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN is empty in production. Add a /api/v1/health check that asserts Sentry hub is bound AND a placeholder breadcrumb can be enqueued.

items.title: CI workflow targets retired 'master' branch
items.evidence: branches: [main, master]. master was retired 2026-05-08 (per project memory). Push events to master can no longer happen, but PR events targeting master would silently skip CI if anyone re-creates the branch.
items.fix: Drop master from the trigger lists. Single-line edit; no runtime impact.

items.title: No migrate --pretend rehearsal in CI — schema-breaking diffs land unverified
items.evidence: CI runs `php artisan migrate --force` on a fresh MySQL 8 service, so green CI proves migrations apply on an empty schema. It does NOT prove they apply on a production-shaped schema (existing data, FK constraints, indexes already present). The earlier MySQL JSON-default + enum-string mismatch pattern (per memory) bit this exact gap.
items.fix: Add a CI step that seeds the test DB with a small representative fixture before migrate (factory: 50 landlords + 1k leases + 5k invoices). Migrate against THAT, not empty. Surfaces NOT NULL backfills + index lock contention before prod.

items.title: No config/route/event cache warming documented for deploy
items.evidence: Laravel cold-start without config:cache + route:cache + event:cache + view:cache pays full reflection cost on every request. Without a deploy script that runs these, a fresh container hits 200-400ms-per-request floor on routes that should be 30ms.
items.fix: Ship deploy.sh in repo root (or scripts/deploy.sh). After migrate: artisan config:cache && route:cache && event:cache && view:cache. Document in README and reference from deploy workflow.

items.title: No queue:restart documented post-deploy — stale workers run old code
items.evidence: Long-running queue workers (or Horizon supervisors) cache class files in opcache and the boot autoloader. Post-deploy, workers continue executing pre-deploy job handlers. A late-fee bug fix won't take effect until next worker restart (could be days under supervisor).
items.fix: deploy.sh must call `php artisan queue:restart` (and `php artisan horizon:terminate` if Horizon is in use). Document the supervisor restart contract in README.

items.title: /up health endpoint is Laravel's built-in static — bypasses real liveness checks
items.evidence: withRouting(health: '/up') registers Laravel's static OK probe. The real probe at /api/v1/health (OBS-2) checks DB + Redis + queue + WebhookDeadLetter depth. A load balancer pointed at /up gets a 200 even when MySQL is wedged.
items.fix: Either point the LB at /api/v1/health, or remove the /up shortcut so there's only one liveness endpoint. Document the chosen LB target in the deploy runbook.

items.title: README has no Deployment section — new ops staff have no source of truth
items.evidence: Memory captures stack + CI gates + migration gotchas, but the public README is the first place an operator will look. Without a 'Deployment' section that lists the cutover steps, env-var inventory, and rollback procedure, runbook lives only in tribal knowledge.
items.fix: Add a Deployment section to README covering: deploy.sh invocation, required production env vars (link to .env.example with a 'prod overrides' comment), rollback (gh deployment list + cherry-pick revert), and on-call escalation.

items.title: validateProductionSecurity warnings have no ops-visible escalation
items.evidence: Warnings land in Log::channel('security'). After OBS-1 ships, security-channel errors flow to Sentry — but warnings DON'T (sentry-laravel default is error+). Misconfig warnings are visible only to whoever tails the log file.
items.fix: Bump from warning to error in the validator, OR add the security channel to the Sentry log channel mapping. Pick the level so misconfig still pages oncall.

items.title: Production-config validator covers 5 of ~90 env vars — most critical creds missing
items.evidence: Validator checks: APP_DEBUG, SESSION_ENCRYPT, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE, HSTS, APP_KEY, REVERB placeholders. .env.example exposes ~90 keys including MAIL_PASSWORD, REDIS_PASSWORD, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, MPESA_CONSUMER_SECRET, PAYSTACK_SECRET_KEY, INTASEND_SECRET_KEY, SAML_SP_*, KENYA_DPA_REGISTRATION. A production deploy that forgets to set any of these ships a broken service or an unauthenticated integration.
items.fix: Extend the validator with a 'required-in-production' env list. Loop over each, log/throw on empty. Categorize by criticality: 'critical' (APP_KEY, DB_PASSWORD, payment gateway creds) -> throw; 'warning' (analytics, optional integrations) -> log.

items.title: APP_DEBUG=true is the .env.example default — a copy-and-deploy leaks stack traces
items.evidence: The current validateProductionSecurity catches APP_DEBUG=true ONLY when APP_ENV is exactly 'production'. If a staging/uat deploy uses APP_ENV=staging, debug stays on and stack traces (with framework-extracted env values) ship to browsers. Defense-in-depth: APP_DEBUG should default to false in the file, with a comment that local dev must opt in.
items.fix: Flip the .env.example default to APP_DEBUG=false. Add comment line explaining local override. Extend validator to throw when APP_DEBUG=true and APP_ENV != 'local'.

items.title: SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN blank in .env.example with no production check
items.evidence: Phase-5 OBS-1 commit c422057 added a blank DSN placeholder (intentional — sentry-laravel no-ops on empty DSN, keeping CI green). The flip side: production with no DSN is also a no-op, defeating the entire OBS-1 effort. There's currently zero signal that prod is mis-deployed.
items.fix: Add a validateProductionSecurity warning (escalating to critical if app.env=production) when SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN is empty. Cross-reference with DEPLOY-3 — same fix.

items.title: Per-landlord webhook secrets (CRYPTO-11) have no rotation procedure
items.evidence: CRYPTO-11 introduced encrypted {coop,equity,kcb}_webhook_secret columns on PaymentConfiguration. APP_KEY rotation has CryptRotate (CRYPTO-6). What's missing: a per-landlord secret rotation flow when a single landlord's secret leaks. Today an ops person has to manually SSH into tinker, encrypt a new secret, write it directly to the DB column. No audit trail, no Slack notification to the affected landlord, no atomic 'old + new both accepted for 24h' transition window.
items.fix: Add `php artisan webhook:rotate-secret {--bank=} {--landlord=}` command. Generates new random secret, encrypts via the model cast, records SecurityLog entry with old-secret hash + new-secret hash + actor. Emits a HasNewWebhookSecret notification to the landlord. Document the 24h cutover assumption (banks will send signed callbacks with the OLD secret until they re-key on their side).

items.title: LOG_LEVEL=debug default leaks SQL queries with PII to logs
items.evidence: Laravel's `debug` level logs every query (when query log is enabled) and every framework breadcrumb. A production server that doesn't override LOG_LEVEL writes SELECT clauses (including bound PII) to log files. PII-in-logs is a Kenya DPA hazard.
items.fix: Set LOG_LEVEL=warning in .env.example. Add a validator warning when LOG_LEVEL is debug or info in production. Keep dev/CI on debug via override.

items.title: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=false default — validator warns instead of fail
items.evidence: Default is false (correct for local dev). Validator warns in prod but doesn't throw. A misconfigured prod with HTTP-only mode ships session cookies in cleartext if HTTPS isn't strictly enforced upstream.
items.fix: Validator should THROW for SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=false in production (not just warn). The Reverb-placeholder code path already shows the throw pattern; mirror it.

items.title: MAIL_MAILER=log default — production deploys silently discard transactional email
items.evidence: Default writes all mail to laravel.log. A prod deploy that forgets to override MAIL_MAILER never sends rent reminders, payment receipts, KYC verification emails. Tenants don't know rent is due. Failure mode is silent (logs grow, but nothing user-visible breaks until the support ticket).
items.fix: Add MAIL_MAILER check to the production validator. If MAIL_MAILER='log' or 'array' in production, throw. Document required MAIL_MAILER='smtp'/'mailgun'/'ses' values in the deploy runbook.

items.title: No .env.production.example template — operators copy dev defaults
items.evidence: Operators bootstrapping a new prod environment copy .env.example because it's the only template. Then they tweak ~30 booleans (APP_DEBUG, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE, SECURITY_HSTS_ENABLED, MAIL_MAILER, LOG_LEVEL, REVERB_*, etc.). Forgetting one is the typical deploy failure mode.
items.fix: Add .env.production.example with prod-safe defaults pre-flipped (APP_DEBUG=false, SECURITY_HSTS_ENABLED=true, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true, LOG_LEVEL=warning, MAIL_MAILER=smtp, etc.). Keep blanks for true secrets. README points new operators at it.

items.title: APP_KEY rotation procedure exists but trigger conditions are undocumented
items.evidence: Phase-4 CRYPTO-6 shipped a `php artisan crypt:rotate {--old-key=}` command for APP_KEY rotation across 7 encrypted column families. No documented trigger: when does ops decide to rotate? On suspected leak? Annually? After offboarding a sysadmin? No runbook, no schedule.
items.fix: Add 'Key Rotation' section to README. Document: trigger conditions (leak detected, scheduled annual, departure of ops with key access), prerequisites (maintenance window, encrypted-columns backup), exact command sequence, post-rotation verification (random sample of decrypted rows). Add cron reminder to scheduler for annual rotation.

items.title: KENYA_DPA_REGISTRATION blank in .env.example with no deploy-time enforcement
items.evidence: Kenya DPA requires a registered Data Controller number on every personal-data-processing entity. The env var exists but is blank. No validator checks it in production. A production launch without the registration is non-compliant.
items.fix: Add KENYA_DPA_REGISTRATION to the production validator's required list. If KENYA_DPA_ENABLED=true (already on by default) AND KENYA_DPA_REGISTRATION is empty AND environment is production, log a critical warning and (optionally) refuse to boot.

items.title: BCRYPT_ROUNDS env value is unbounded — a regressed value silently weakens password hashing
items.evidence: BCRYPT_ROUNDS=12 is OK. Nothing prevents a careless operator from setting it to 10 (or 8) and weakening the hashing factor. Hash::driver() uses whatever's in env without sanity bounds.
items.fix: Validator asserts BCRYPT_ROUNDS >= 12 in production. Reject lower at boot. Document 12 as the floor.

phase_1.title: Critical: tighten the production-config validator (fail-closed instead of warn-and-pray)
phase_1.scope: DEPLOY-2, DEPLOY-3, SECRETS-1, SECRETS-2, SECRETS-3, SECRETS-6, SECRETS-7
phase_1.rationale: Single file edit cluster (AppServiceProvider::validateProductionSecurity + .env.example defaults). All HIGH. Highest leverage: a production deploy that's missing APP_KEY/MAIL_MAILER/SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE/Sentry DSN should refuse to start, not log a warning into a file no one watches.

phase_2.title: Critical: deploy automation + secret rotation procedures
phase_2.rationale: Deploy workflow + the two missing rotation procedures (per-landlord webhook secret, APP_KEY trigger conditions). Larger commits than phase 1 but orthogonal — can land in parallel.

phase_3.title: Medium: CI rehearsal + cache warming + worker restart
phase_3.rationale: Each individually small. Bundle into a single deploy.sh + ci.yml refactor.

phase_5.title: LOW: documentation + compliance
phase_5.rationale: README sections + a few BCRYPT_ROUNDS + KENYA_DPA_REGISTRATION validator checks.

out_of_scope: Backup / DR procedure audit — own phase (proposed Phase 12)
Kenya DPA / GDPR coverage (consent UX, retention enforcement, export/erase completeness) — own phase
Dependency supply-chain audit beyond CVE bumps (lockfile freshness, abandoned packages, transitive risk) — own phase
Already-closed CRYPTO-12 Reverb placeholder check — Phase-5 work
